verses.latin_vulgate: In anno secundo Joas filii Joachaz regis Israël, regnavit Amasias filius Joas regis Juda.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בִּשְׁנַ֣ת שְׁתַּ֔יִם לְיוֹאָ֥שׁ בֶּן־יוֹאָחָ֖ז מֶ֣לֶךְ יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל מָלַ֛ךְ אֲמַצְיָ֥הוּ בֶן־יוֹאָ֖שׁ מֶ֥לֶךְ יְהוּדָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: In the second year of Joas son of Joachaz, king of Israel, reigned Amasias son of Joas, king of Juda.
verses.finnish_biblia: Toisena Joaksen Joahaksen pojan Israelin kuninkaan vuonna tuli Amatsia Joaksen Juudan kuninkaan poika kuninkaaksi.
verses.king_james: In the second year of Joash son of Jehoahaz king of Israel reigned Amaziah the son of Joash king of Judah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Viginti quinque annorum erat cum regnare cœpisset : viginti autem et novem annis regnavit in Jerusalem. Nomen matris ejus Joadan de Jerusalem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בֶּן־עֶשְׂרִ֨ים וְחָמֵ֤שׁ שָׁנָה֙ הָיָ֣ה בְמׇלְכ֔וֹ וְעֶשְׂרִ֤ים וָתֵ֙שַׁע֙ שָׁנָ֔ה מָלַ֖ךְ בִּירוּשָׁלָ֑͏ִם וְשֵׁ֣ם אִמּ֔וֹ (יהועדין) [יְהוֹעַדָּ֖ן] מִן־יְרוּשָׁלָֽ͏ִם׃
verses.douay_rheims: He was five and twenty years old when he began to reign; and nine and twenty years he reigned in Jerusalem; the name of his mother was Joadan, of Jerusalem.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän oli viiden ajastajan vanha kolmattakymmentä, kuin hän tuli kuninkaaksi, ja hallitsi yhdeksän ajastaikaa kolmattakymmentä Jerusalemissa. Hänen äitinsä nimi oli Joaddan Jerusalemista.
verses.king_james: He was twenty and five years old when he began to reign, and reigned twenty and nine years in Jerusalem. And his mother’s name was Jehoaddan of Jerusalem.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et fecit rectum coram Domino, verumtamen non ut David pater ejus. Juxta omnia quæ fecit Joas pater suus, fecit :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֤עַשׂ הַיָּשָׁר֙ בְּעֵינֵ֣י יְהֹוָ֔ה רַ֕ק לֹ֖א כְּדָוִ֣ד אָבִ֑יו כְּכֹ֧ל אֲשֶׁר־עָשָׂ֛ה יוֹאָ֥שׁ אָבִ֖יו עָשָֽׂה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he did that which was right before the Lord, but yet not like David his father. He did according to all things that Joas his father, did:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän teki mitä Herralle hyvästi kelpasi, ei kuitenkaan niinkuin hänen isänsä David, vaan kaiketi niinkuin hänen isänsä Joas oli tehnyt, niin myös hän teki.
verses.king_james: And he did that which was right in the sight of the Lord, yet not like David his father: he did according to all things as Joash his father did.

verses.latin_vulgate: nisi hoc tantum, quod excelsa non abstulit : adhuc enim populus immolabat, et adolebat incensum in excelsis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רַ֥ק הַבָּמ֖וֹת לֹא־סָ֑רוּ ע֥וֹד הָעָ֛ם מְזַבְּחִ֥ים וּֽמְקַטְּרִ֖ים בַּבָּמֽוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: But this only, that he took not away the high places; for yet the people sacrificed, and burnt incense in the high places:
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä korkeudet ei olleet pannut pois; mutta kansa uhrasi vielä ja suitsutti korkeuksilla.
verses.king_james: Howbeit the high places were not taken away: as yet the people did sacrifice and burnt incense on the high places.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque obtinuisset regnum, percussit servos suos, qui interfecerant regem patrem suum :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֕י כַּאֲשֶׁ֛ר חָזְקָ֥ה הַמַּמְלָכָ֖ה בְּיָד֑וֹ וַיַּךְ֙ אֶת־עֲבָדָ֔יו הַמַּכִּ֖ים אֶת־הַמֶּ֥לֶךְ אָבִֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when he had possession of the kingdom, he put his servants to death that had slain the king, his father.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin valtakunta hänen kädessänsä vahvistui, löi hän palveliansa, jotka kuninkaan hänen isänsä tappaneet olivat.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, as soon as the kingdom was confirmed in his hand, that he slew his servants which had slain the king his father.

verses.latin_vulgate: filios autem eorum qui occiderant, non occidit, juxta quod scriptum est in libro legis Moysi, sicut præcepit Dominus, dicens : Non morientur patres pro filiis, neque filii morientur pro patribus : sed unusquisque in peccato suo morietur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאֶת־בְּנֵ֥י הַמַּכִּ֖ים לֹ֣א הֵמִ֑ית כַּכָּת֣וּב בְּסֵֽפֶר־תּֽוֹרַת־מֹ֠שֶׁ֠ה אֲשֶׁר־צִוָּ֨ה יְהֹוָ֜ה לֵאמֹ֗ר לֹא־יוּמְת֨וּ אָב֤וֹת עַל־בָּנִים֙ וּבָנִים֙ לֹא־יוּמְת֣וּ עַל־אָב֔וֹת כִּ֛י אִם־אִ֥ישׁ בְּחֶטְא֖וֹ (ימות) [יוּמָֽת]׃
verses.douay_rheims: But the children of the murderers he did not put to death, according to that which is written in the book of the law of Moses, wherein the Lord commanded, saying: The fathers shall not be put to death for the children, neither shall the children be put to death for the fathers: but every man shall die for his own sin.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta tappajain lapsia ei hän surmannut, niinkuin kirjoitettu on Moseksen lakikirjassa, jossa Herra käskenyt oli ja sanonut: Isät ei pidä kuoleman lasten tähden, ja lapset ei pidä kuoleman isäinsä tähden, vaan jokainen pitää kuoleman oman syntinsä tähden.
verses.king_james: But the children of the murderers he slew not: according unto that which is written in the book of the law of Moses, wherein the Lord commanded, saying, The fathers shall not be put to death for the children, nor the children be put to death for the fathers; but every man shall be put to death for his own sin.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ipse percussit Edom in valle Salinarum decem millia, et apprehendit petram in prælio, vocavitque nomen ejus Jectehel usque in præsentem diem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הוּא־הִכָּ֨ה אֶת־אֱד֤וֹם בְּגֵי־[מֶ֙לַח֙] (המלח) עֲשֶׂ֣רֶת אֲלָפִ֔ים וְתָפַ֥שׂ אֶת־הַסֶּ֖לַע בַּמִּלְחָמָ֑ה וַיִּקְרָ֤א אֶת־שְׁמָהּ֙ יׇקְתְאֵ֔ל עַ֖ד הַיּ֥וֹם הַזֶּֽה׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: He slew of Edom in the valley of the Saltpits, ten thousand men, and took the rock by war, and called the name thereof Jectehel, unto this day.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän löi myös kymmenentuhatta Edomilaista Suolalaaksossa, ja voitti Selan (kaupungin) sodalla, ja kutsui sen nimen Jokteel tähän päivään asti.
verses.king_james: He slew of Edom in the valley of salt ten thousand, and took Selah by war, and called the name of it Joktheel unto this day.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc misit Amasias nuntios ad Joas filium Joachaz filii Jehu regis Israël, dicens : Veni, et videamus nos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָ֣ז שָׁלַ֤ח אֲמַצְיָה֙ מַלְאָכִ֔ים אֶל־יְהוֹאָ֨שׁ בֶּן־יְהוֹאָחָ֧ז בֶּן־יֵה֛וּא מֶ֥לֶךְ יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל לֵאמֹ֑ר לְכָ֖ה נִתְרָאֶ֥ה פָנִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then Amasias sent messengers to Joas, son of Joachaz, son of Jehu, king of Israel, saying: Come, let us see one another.
verses.finnish_biblia: Silloin lähetti Amatsia sanansaattajat Joaksen Joahaksen pojan Jehun pojan Israelin kuninkaan tykö, ja käski hänelle sanoa: tule ja katselkaamme toinen toistamme!
verses.king_james: Then Amaziah sent messengers to Jehoash, the son of Jehoahaz son of Jehu, king of Israel, saying, Come, let us look one another in the face.

verses.latin_vulgate: Remisitque Joas rex Israël ad Amasiam regem Juda, dicens : Carduus Libani misit ad cedrum quæ est in Libano, dicens : Da filiam tuam filio meo uxorem. Transieruntque bestiæ saltus quæ sunt in Libano, et conculcaverunt carduum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁלַ֞ח יְהוֹאָ֣שׁ מֶלֶךְ־יִשְׂרָאֵ֗ל אֶל־אֲמַצְיָ֣הוּ מֶלֶךְ־יְהוּדָה֮ לֵאמֹר֒ הַח֜וֹחַ אֲשֶׁ֣ר בַּלְּבָנ֗וֹן שָׁ֠לַ֠ח אֶל־הָאֶ֜רֶז אֲשֶׁ֤ר בַּלְּבָנוֹן֙ לֵאמֹ֔ר תְּנָה־אֶת־בִּתְּךָ֥ לִבְנִ֖י לְאִשָּׁ֑ה וַֽתַּעֲבֹ֞ר חַיַּ֤ת הַשָּׂדֶה֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר בַּלְּבָנ֔וֹן וַתִּרְמֹ֖ס אֶת־הַחֽוֹחַ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Joas, king of Israel, sent again to Amasias, king of Juda, saying: A thistle of Libanus sent to a cedar tree, which is in Libanus, saying: Give thy daughter to my son to wife. And the beasts of the forest, that are in Libanus, passed, and trod down the thistle.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Joas Israelin kuningas lähetti Amatsian Juudan kuninkaan tykö ja antoi hänelle sanoa: orjantappura, Libanonissa, lähetti sedripuun tykö Libanonissa ja antoi hänelle sanoa: anna tyttäres minun pojalleni emännäksi! Mutta metsän eläimet kedolla Libanonissa juoksivat orjantappuran päälle ja maahan tallasivat sen.
verses.king_james: And Jehoash the king of Israel sent to Amaziah king of Judah, saying, The thistle that was in Lebanon sent to the cedar that was in Lebanon, saying, Give thy daughter to my son to wife: and there passed by a wild beast that was in Lebanon, and trode down the thistle.

verses.latin_vulgate: Percutiens invaluisti super Edom, et sublevavit te cor tuum : contentus esto gloria, et sede in domo tua : quare provocas malum, ut cadas tu et Judas tecum ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַכֵּ֤ה הִכִּ֙יתָ֙ אֶת־אֱד֔וֹם וּֽנְשָׂאֲךָ֖ לִבֶּ֑ךָ הִכָּבֵד֙ וְשֵׁ֣ב בְּבֵיתֶ֔ךָ וְלָ֤מָּה תִתְגָּרֶה֙ בְּרָעָ֔ה וְנָ֣פַלְתָּ֔ה אַתָּ֖ה וִיהוּדָ֥ה עִמָּֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Thou hast beaten and prevailed over Edom, and thy heart hath lifted thee up; be content with this glory, and sit at home; why provokest thou evil, that thou shouldst fall, and Juda with thee?
verses.finnish_biblia: Sinä olet kokonansa lyönyt Edomilaiset, siitä paisuu sinun sydämes: pidä se ylistys ja ole kotonas! miksis etsit vahinkoa kaatuakses sekä itse että Juuda sinun kanssas?
verses.king_james: Thou hast indeed smitten Edom, and thine heart hath lifted thee up: glory of this, and tarry at home: for why shouldest thou meddle to thy hurt, that thou shouldest fall, even thou, and Judah with thee?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et non acquievit Amasias. Ascenditque Joas rex Israël, et viderunt se, ipse et Amasias rex Juda, in Bethsames oppido Judæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלֹא־שָׁמַ֣ע אֲמַצְיָ֔הוּ וַיַּ֨עַל יְהוֹאָ֤שׁ מֶֽלֶךְ־יִשְׂרָאֵל֙ וַיִּתְרָא֣וּ פָנִ֔ים ה֖וּא וַאֲמַצְיָ֣הוּ מֶלֶךְ־יְהוּדָ֑ה בְּבֵ֥ית שֶׁ֖מֶשׁ אֲשֶׁ֥ר לִיהוּדָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: But Amasias did not rest satisfied. So Joas, king of Israel, went up; and he and Amasias, king of Juda, saw one another in Bethsames, a town in Juda.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Amatsia ei totellut häntä. Niin meni Joas Israelin kuningas ylös, ja he katselivat toinen toistansa, hän ja Amatsia Juudan kuningas Betsemeksessä, joka Juudassa on.
verses.king_james: But Amaziah would not hear. Therefore Jehoash king of Israel went up; and he and Amaziah king of Judah looked one another in the face at Beth–shemesh, which belongeth to Judah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Percussusque est Juda coram Israël, et fugerunt unusquisque in tabernacula sua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּנָּ֥גֶף יְהוּדָ֖ה לִפְנֵ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל וַיָּנֻ֖סוּ אִ֥ישׁ לְאֹהָלָֽו׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Juda was put to the worse before Israel, and they fled every man to their dwellings.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Juuda lyötiin Israelilta, niin että jokainen pakeni majoillensa.
verses.king_james: And Judah was put to the worse before Israel; and they fled every man to their tents.

verses.latin_vulgate: Amasiam vero regem Juda, filium Joas filii Ochoziæ, cepit Joas rex Israël in Bethsames, et adduxit eum in Jerusalem : et interrupit murum Jerusalem, a porta Ephraim usque ad portam anguli, quadringentis cubitis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאֵת֩ אֲמַצְיָ֨הוּ מֶלֶךְ־יְהוּדָ֜ה בֶּן־יְהוֹאָ֣שׁ בֶּן־אֲחַזְיָ֗הוּ תָּפַ֛שׂ יְהוֹאָ֥שׁ מֶלֶךְ־יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל בְּבֵ֣ית שָׁ֑מֶשׁ (ויבאו) [וַיָּבֹא֙] יְר֣וּשָׁלַ֔͏ִם וַיִּפְרֹץ֩ בְּחוֹמַ֨ת יְרוּשָׁלַ֜͏ִם בְּשַׁ֤עַר אֶפְרַ֙יִם֙ עַד־שַׁ֣עַר הַפִּנָּ֔ה אַרְבַּ֥ע מֵא֖וֹת אַמָּֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: But Joas, king of Israel, took Amasias, king of Juda, the son of Joas, the son of Ochozias, in Bethsames, and brought him into Jerusalem; and he broke down the wall of Jerusalem, from the gate of Ephraim to the gate of the corner, four hundred cubits.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Joas Israelin kuningas otti Amatsian Juudan kuninkaan Joaksen pojan, Ahasian pojan, Betsemeksessä kiinni, ja tuli Jerusalemiin, ja kukisti Jerusalemin muurit Ephraimin portista Kulmaporttiin asti, neljänsadan kyynärän pituudelta,
verses.king_james: And Jehoash king of Israel took Amaziah king of Judah, the son of Jehoash the son of Ahaziah, at Beth–shemesh, and came to Jerusalem, and brake down the wall of Jerusalem from the gate of Ephraim unto the corner gate, four hundred cubits.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tulitque omne aurum et argentum, et universa vasa quæ inventa sunt in domo Domini et in thesauris regis, et obsides, et reversus est in Samariam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלָקַ֣ח אֶת־כׇּל־הַזָּֽהָב־וְ֠הַכֶּ֠סֶף וְאֵ֨ת כׇּל־הַכֵּלִ֜ים הַנִּמְצְאִ֣ים בֵּית־יְהֹוָ֗ה וּבְאֹֽצְרוֹת֙ בֵּ֣ית הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ וְאֵ֖ת בְּנֵ֣י הַתַּעֲרֻב֑וֹת וַיָּ֖שׇׁב שֹׁמְרֽוֹנָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he took all the gold and silver, and all the vessels that were found in the house of the Lord, and in the king's treasures, and hostages, and returned to Samaria.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja otti kaiken kullan ja hopian ja kaikki astiat, jotka löyttiin Herran huoneesta ja kuninkaan huoneen tavaroista, ja vielä hänen lapsensa pantiksi, ja palasi Samariaan.
verses.king_james: And he took all the gold and silver, and all the vessels that were found in the house of the Lord, and in the treasures of the king’s house, and hostages, and returned to Samaria.

verses.latin_vulgate: Reliqua autem verborum Joas quæ fecit, et fortitudo ejus qua pugnavit contra Amasiam regem Juda, nonne hæc scripta sunt in libro sermonum dierum regum Israël ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיֶ֩תֶר֩ דִּבְרֵ֨י יְהוֹאָ֜שׁ אֲשֶׁ֤ר עָשָׂה֙ וּגְב֣וּרָת֔וֹ וַאֲשֶׁ֣ר נִלְחַ֔ם עִ֖ם אֲמַצְיָ֣הוּ מֶלֶךְ־יְהוּדָ֑ה הֲלֹא־הֵ֣ם כְּתוּבִ֗ים עַל־סֵ֛פֶר דִּבְרֵ֥י הַיָּמִ֖ים לְמַלְכֵ֥י יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: But the rest of the acts of Joas, which he did, and his valour, wherewith he fought against Amasias, king of Juda, are they not written in the book of the words of the days of the kings of Israel?
verses.finnish_biblia: Mitä enempi Joaksesta sanomista on, mitä hän tehnyt on, ja hänen väkevyydestänsä, ja kuinka hän Amatsian Juudan kuninkaan kanssa soti: eikö se ole kirjoitettu Israelin kuningasten aikakirjassa?
verses.king_james: Now the rest of the acts of Jehoash which he did, and his might, and how he fought with Amaziah king of Judah, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dormivitque Joas cum patribus suis, et sepultus est in Samaria cum regibus Israël, et regnavit Jeroboam filius ejus pro eo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁכַּ֤ב יְהוֹאָשׁ֙ עִם־אֲבֹתָ֔יו וַיִּקָּבֵר֙ בְּשֹׁ֣מְר֔וֹן עִ֖ם מַלְכֵ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל וַיִּמְלֹ֛ךְ יָרׇבְעָ֥ם בְּנ֖וֹ תַּחְתָּֽיו׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And Joas slept with his fathers, and was buried in Samaria, with the kings of Israel: and Jeroboam, his son, reigned in his stead.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Joas nukkui isäinsä kanssa ja haudattiin Samariassa, Israelin kuningasten sekaan; ja hänen poikansa Jerobeam tuli kuninkaaksi hänen siaansa.
verses.king_james: And Jehoash slept with his fathers, and was buried in Samaria with the kings of Israel; and Jeroboam his son reigned in his stead.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vixit autem Amasias filius Joas rex Juda postquam mortuus est Joas filius Joachaz regis Israël, quindecim annis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְחִ֨י אֲמַצְיָ֤הוּ בֶן־יוֹאָשׁ֙ מֶ֣לֶךְ יְהוּדָ֔ה אַחֲרֵ֣י מ֔וֹת יְהוֹאָ֥שׁ בֶּן־יְהוֹאָחָ֖ז מֶ֣לֶךְ יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל חֲמֵ֥שׁ עֶשְׂרֵ֖ה שָׁנָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Amasias, the son of Joas, king of Juda, lived after the death of Joas, son of Joachaz, king of Israel, fifteen years.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Amatsia, Joaksen Juudan kuninkaan poika, eli Joaksen Joahaksen Israelin kuninkaan pojan kuoleman jälkeen viisitoistakymmentä ajastaikaa.
verses.king_james: And Amaziah the son of Joash king of Judah lived after the death of Jehoash son of Jehoahaz king of Israel fifteen years.

verses.latin_vulgate: Reliqua autem sermonum Amasiæ, nonne hæc scripta sunt in libro sermonum dierum regum Juda ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיֶ֖תֶר דִּבְרֵ֣י אֲמַצְיָ֑הוּ הֲלֹא־הֵ֣ם כְּתֻבִ֗ים עַל־סֵ֛פֶר דִּבְרֵ֥י הַיָּמִ֖ים לְמַלְכֵ֥י יְהוּדָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the rest of the acts of Amasias, are they not written in the book of the words of the days of the kings of Juda?
verses.finnish_biblia: Mitä enempi Amatsiasta sanomista on: eikö se ole kirjoitettu Juudan kuningasten aikakirjassa?
verses.king_james: And the rest of the acts of Amaziah, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?

verses.latin_vulgate: Factaque est contra eum conjuratio in Jerusalem : at ille fugit in Lachis. Miseruntque post eum in Lachis, et interfecerunt eum ibi :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקְשְׁר֨וּ עָלָ֥יו קֶ֛שֶׁר בִּירוּשָׁלַ֖͏ִם וַיָּ֣נׇס לָכִ֑ישָׁה וַיִּשְׁלְח֤וּ אַחֲרָיו֙ לָכִ֔ישָׁה וַיְמִתֻ֖הוּ שָֽׁם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now they made a conspiracy against him in Jerusalem: and he fled to Lachis. And they sent after him to Lachis, and killed him there.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja he tekivät liiton häntä vastaan Jerusalemissa; mutta hän pakeni Lakikseen, ja he lähettivät hänen peräänsä Lakikseen, ja tappoivat hänen siellä.
verses.king_james: Now they made a conspiracy against him in Jerusalem: and he fled to Lachish; but they sent after him to Lachish, and slew him there.

verses.latin_vulgate: et asportaverunt in equis, sepultusque est in Jerusalem cum patribus suis in civitate David.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׂא֥וּ אֹת֖וֹ עַל־הַסּוּסִ֑ים וַיִּקָּבֵ֧ר בִּירוּשָׁלַ֛͏ִם עִם־אֲבֹתָ֖יו בְּעִ֥יר דָּוִֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they brought him away upon horses, and he was buried in Jerusalem with his fathers, in the city of David.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja he veivät hänen sieltä hevosilla pois, ja hän haudattiin Jerusalemissa isäinsä oheen Davidin kaupungissa.
verses.king_james: And they brought him on horses: and he was buried at Jerusalem with his fathers in the city of David.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tulit autem universus populus Judæ Azariam annos natum sedecim, et constituerunt eum regem pro patre ejus Amasia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקְח֞וּ כׇּל־עַ֤ם יְהוּדָה֙ אֶת־עֲזַרְיָ֔ה וְה֕וּא בֶּן־שֵׁ֥שׁ עֶשְׂרֵ֖ה שָׁנָ֑ה וַיַּמְלִ֣כוּ אֹת֔וֹ תַּ֖חַת אָבִ֥יו אֲמַצְיָֽהוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And all the people of Juda took Azarias, who was sixteen years old, and made him king instead of his father, Amasias.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja koko Juudan kansa ottivat Asarian kuudentoistakymmenen ajastajan vanhana, ja tekivät hänen kuninkaaksi isänsä Amatsian siaan.
verses.king_james: And all the people of Judah took Azariah, which was sixteen years old, and made him king instead of his father Amaziah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ipse ædificavit Ælath, et restituit eam Judæ, postquam dormivit rex cum patribus suis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ה֚וּא בָּנָ֣ה אֶת־אֵילַ֔ת וַיְשִׁבֶ֖הָ לִֽיהוּדָ֑ה אַחֲרֵ֥י שְׁכַֽב־הַמֶּ֖לֶךְ עִם־אֲבֹתָֽיו׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: He built Elath, and restored it to Juda, after that the king slept with his fathers.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän rakensi Elatin ja saatti sen jälleen Juudan alle, sitte kuin kuningas isäinsä kanssa nukkunut oli.
verses.king_james: He built Elath, and restored it to Judah, after that the king slept with his fathers.

verses.latin_vulgate: Anno quintodecimo Amasiæ filii Joas regis Juda, regnavit Jeroboam filius Joas regis Israël in Samaria, quadraginta et uno anno.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בִּשְׁנַת֙ חֲמֵשׁ־עֶשְׂרֵ֣ה שָׁנָ֔ה לַאֲמַצְיָ֥הוּ בֶן־יוֹאָ֖שׁ מֶ֣לֶךְ יְהוּדָ֑ה מָ֠לַ֠ךְ יָרׇבְעָ֨ם בֶּן־יוֹאָ֤שׁ מֶֽלֶךְ־יִשְׂרָאֵל֙ בְּשֹׁ֣מְר֔וֹן אַרְבָּעִ֥ים וְאַחַ֖ת שָׁנָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: In the fifteenth year of Amasias, son of Joas, king of Juda, reigned Jeroboam, the son of Joas, king of Israel, in Samaria, one and forty years:
verses.finnish_biblia: Amatsian Joaksen pojan Juudan kuninkaan viidentenä vuonna toistakymmentä oli Jerobeam Joaksen poika Israelin kuningas Samariassa yhden ajastajan viidettäkymmentä,
verses.king_james: In the fifteenth year of Amaziah the son of Joash king of Judah Jeroboam the son of Joash king of Israel began to reign in Samaria, and reigned forty and one years.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et fecit quod malum est coram Domino : non recessit ab omnibus peccatis Jeroboam filii Nabat, qui peccare fecit Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֥עַשׂ הָרַ֖ע בְּעֵינֵ֣י יְהֹוָ֑ה לֹ֣א סָ֗ר מִכׇּל־חַטֹּאות֙ יָרׇבְעָ֣ם בֶּן־נְבָ֔ט אֲשֶׁ֥ר הֶחֱטִ֖יא אֶת־יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he did that which is evil before the Lord. He departed not from all the sins of Jeroboam, the son of Nabat, who made Israel to sin.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja teki pahaa Herran edessä, ja ei lakannut kaikista Jerobeamin Nebatin pojan synneistä, joka Israelin saatti syntiä tekemään.
verses.king_james: And he did that which was evil in the sight of the Lord: he departed not from all the sins of Jeroboam the son of Nebat, who made Israel to sin.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ipse restituit terminos Israël ab introitu Emath usque ad mare solitudinis, juxta sermonem Domini Dei Israël quem locutus est per servum suum Jonam filium Amathi prophetam, qui erat de Geth, quæ est in Opher.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ה֗וּא הֵשִׁיב֙ אֶת־גְּב֣וּל יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל מִלְּב֥וֹא חֲמָ֖ת עַד־יָ֣ם הָעֲרָבָ֑ה כִּדְבַ֤ר יְהֹוָה֙ אֱלֹהֵ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל אֲשֶׁ֣ר דִּבֶּ֗ר בְּיַד־עַבְדּ֞וֹ יוֹנָ֤ה בֶן־אֲמִתַּי֙ הַנָּבִ֔יא אֲשֶׁ֖ר מִגַּ֥ת הַחֵֽפֶר׃
verses.douay_rheims: He restored the borders of Israel from the entrance of Emath, unto the sea of the wilderness, according to the word of the Lord, the God of Israel, which he spoke by his servant, Jonas, the son of Amathi, the prophet, who was of Geth, which is in Opher.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän otti Israelin maan rajat jälleen, Hematista hamaan kedon mereen asti, Herran Israelin Jumalan sanan jälkeen, jonka hän sanonut oli palveliansa, propheta Jonan, Amittain pojan kautta, joka oli GatHepheristä.
verses.king_james: He restored the coast of Israel from the entering of Hamath unto the sea of the plain, according to the word of the Lord God of Israel, which he spake by the hand of his servant Jonah, the son of Amittai, the prophet, which was of Gath–hepher.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vidit enim Dominus afflictionem Israël amaram nimis, et quod consumpti essent usque ad clausos carcere et extremos, et non esset qui auxiliaretur Israëli.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּי־רָאָ֧ה יְהֹוָ֛ה אֶת־עֳנִ֥י יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל מֹרֶ֣ה מְאֹ֑ד וְאֶ֤פֶס עָצוּר֙ וְאֶ֣פֶס עָז֔וּב וְאֵ֥ין עֹזֵ֖ר לְיִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: For the Lord saw the affliction of Israel, that it was exceedingly bitter, and that they were consumed even to them that were shut up in prison, and the lowest persons, and that there was no one to help Israel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä Herra näki Israelin suuren viheliäisyyden, niin ettei ollut suljettua, eikä hylättyä, eikä auttajaa Israelissa.
verses.king_james: For the Lord saw the affliction of Israel, that it was very bitter: for there was not any shut up, nor any left, nor any helper for Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nec locutus est Dominus ut deleret nomen Israël de sub cælo, sed salvavit eos in manu Jeroboam filii Joas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלֹא־דִבֶּ֣ר יְהֹוָ֔ה לִמְחוֹת֙ אֶת־שֵׁ֣ם יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל מִתַּ֖חַת הַשָּׁמָ֑יִם וַיּ֣וֹשִׁיעֵ֔ם בְּיַ֖ד יָרׇבְעָ֥ם בֶּן־יוֹאָֽשׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the Lord did not say that he would blot out the name of Israel from under heaven; but he saved them by the hand of Jeroboam, the son of Joas.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Herra ei ollut sanonut tahtovansa pyyhkiä Israelin nimeä taivaan alta pois; mutta autti heitä Jerobeamin Joaksen pojan kautta.
verses.king_james: And the Lord said not that he would blot out the name of Israel from under heaven: but he saved them by the hand of Jeroboam the son of Joash.

verses.latin_vulgate: Reliqua autem sermonum Jeroboam, et universa quæ fecit, et fortitudo ejus qua præliatus est, et quomodo restituit Damascum et Emath Judæ in Israël, nonne hæc scripta sunt in libro sermonum dierum regum Israël ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיֶ֩תֶר֩ דִּבְרֵ֨י יָרׇבְעָ֜ם וְכׇל־אֲשֶׁ֤ר עָשָׂה֙ וּגְבוּרָת֣וֹ אֲשֶׁר־נִלְחָ֔ם וַאֲשֶׁ֨ר הֵשִׁ֜יב אֶת־דַּמֶּ֧שֶׂק וְאֶת־חֲמָ֛ת לִיהוּדָ֖ה בְּיִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל הֲלֹא־הֵ֣ם כְּתוּבִ֗ים עַל־סֵ֛פֶר דִּבְרֵ֥י הַיָּמִ֖ים לְמַלְכֵ֥י יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: But the rest of the acts of Jeroboam, and all that he did, and his valour, wherewith he fought, and how he restored Damascus and Emath to Juda, in Israel, are they not written in the book of the words of the days of the kings of Israel?
verses.finnish_biblia: Mitä enempi Jerobeamista sanomista on, ja kaikista mitä hän tehnyt on, ja hänen väkevyydestänsä, kuinka hän sotinut on, ja kuinka hän otti Damaskun jälleen ja Hematin Juudalle Israelissa: eikö se ole kirjoitettu Israelin kuningasten aikakirjassa?
verses.king_james: Now the rest of the acts of Jeroboam, and all that he did, and his might, how he warred, and how he recovered Damascus, and Hamath, which belonged to Judah, for Israel, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dormivitque Jeroboam cum patribus suis regibus Israël, et regnavit Zacharias filius ejus pro eo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁכַּ֤ב יָֽרׇבְעָם֙ עִם־אֲבֹתָ֔יו עִ֖ם מַלְכֵ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל וַיִּמְלֹ֛ךְ זְכַרְיָ֥ה בְנ֖וֹ תַּחְתָּֽיו׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And Jeroboam slept with his fathers, the kings of Israel; and Zacharias, his son, reigned in his stead.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Jerobeam nukkui isäinsä, Israelin kuningasten kanssa; ja hänen poikansa Sakaria tuli kuninkaaksi hänen siaansa.
verses.king_james: And Jeroboam slept with his fathers, even with the kings of Israel; and Zachariah his son reigned in his stead.
